content: Hey, I've been trying to get a better handle on my finances lately. I feel like I'm just winging it month to month.
That's a great goal to work toward. What aspects of your finances are you most concerned about right now?
Mainly just tracking my spending. I'm a Senior Software Engineer at Nexus Labs in San Diego, so I make decent money, but I never seem to have much left at the end of the month.
San Diego can be expensive. Do you rent or own your place?
I rent an apartment with two roommates. We split everything three ways, which helps. But I still feel like my money disappears.
Roommates definitely help with costs. What do you think you spend the most on each month?
Probably food and going out. I love baking sourdough bread as a hobby, so I'm always buying different flours and ingredients. And I hit up local breweries pretty often with friends.
Those hobbies sound fun! Have you tried tracking your expenses for a month to see where your money actually goes?
Not really. I know I should. I just downloaded a budgeting app actually. I'm hoping it'll help me see the big picture.
That's a great first step. The app can show you patterns you might not notice otherwise. Do you have any financial goals you're working toward?
Yeah, I'd love to buy a house eventually, maybe in a few years. And I want to take a trip to Japan to visit some of the places I've read about in sci-fi novels.
Japan would be amazing! Both of those goals are totally achievable with some planning. Have you thought about setting up automatic savings for them?
I haven't, but that makes sense. I also need to start contributing more to my 401k. I'm only putting in the minimum now.
Increasing your 401k contributions, even by a few percent, can make a big difference over time thanks to compound interest. Would you like some strategies for balancing multiple financial goals?
That would be really helpful actually. I also need to figure out how to budget for my other hobbies - I do archery once a week and I'm trying to get better at gardening too.
It's great that you have hobbies that keep you active and creative. We can definitely work on a plan that includes money for the things you enjoy while still making progress on your bigger goals.
Thanks, I appreciate that. I guess I just need to be more intentional about where my money goes instead of wondering where it all went.
That's exactly right. Small changes in awareness can lead to big improvements over time. Want to walk through setting up your first budget categories?
